title: Slice Operations
description: How to add items and find the length.

slides.title: The 'append' Function ➕
slides.content:
## Growing the Slice

To add a new item to the end of a slice, Go provides a built-in function called **`append()`**.

Because slices are dynamic, when you append an item, Go automatically expands the slice in memory to make room!

slides.title: Re-assigning the Slice 🔄
slides.content: Crucially, `append()` creates a *new*, larger slice. You must re-assign it back to the original variable.

quizData.question: What does the `append()` function do?

options.text: Removes the last item of a slice
Adds new elements to the end of a slice

quizData.explanation: The `append` function grows the slice by pushing elements sequentially onto its tail.

slides.title: The 'len' Function 📏
slides.content:
## How Big is It?

If you ever need to know exactly how many items are currently inside your slice (or array), use the built-in **`len()`** function.

`count := len(names)`

quizData.question: To get the count or length of items in a slice, wrap the slice variable physically inside the `____()` function.
quizData.explanation: The abbreviation `len` perfectly identifies the length of a slice in Go.
